root_causes.cause: R2 has an inbound ACL on Serial0/1/0 that explicitly denies OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving R1's OSPF hellos and blocking adjacency formation.
evidence: R2 running-config: Serial0/1/0 shows an access-group inbound: 'access-group: in -> acl-name: R2_IN' (interface Serial0/1/0).
R2 ACL 'R2_IN' contains sequence 10: 'deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5' and sequence 20: 'deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6' (these are OSPF multicast addresses).
R2 OSPF output: Serial0/1/0 interface has no neighbors (neighbors: []).
R1 OSPF output: Serial0/1/0 shows neighbor 2.2.2.2 (10.0.0.2) in state 'ospf-nbr-init' (R1 is receiving R2's hellos but R2 is not receiving R1's hellos).

root_causes.cause: R2 has an inbound ACL on Serial0/1/0 that explicitly denies OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving R1's OSPF hellos and blocking adjacency formation.
evidence: R2 running-config: Serial0/1/0 shows an access-group inbound: 'access-group: in -> acl-name: R2_IN' (interface Serial0/1/0).
R2 ACL 'R2_IN' contains sequence 10: 'deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5' and sequence 20: 'deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6' (these are OSPF multicast addresses).

root_causes.cause: R2 has an inbound ACL on Serial0/1/0 that explicitly denies OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving R1's OSPF hellos and blocking adjacency formation.
evidence: R2 running-config: Serial0/1/0 shows an access-group inbound: 'access-group: in -> acl-name: R2_IN' (interface Serial0/1/0).
R2 ACL 'R2_IN' contains sequence 10: 'deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5' and sequence 20: 'deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6' (these are OSPF multicast addresses).

R1 OSPF output: Serial0/1/0 shows neighbor 2.2.2.2 (10.0.0.2) in state 'ospf-nbr-init' (R1 is receiving R2's hellos but R2 is not receiving R1's hellos).

data.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on R2 is down because inbound ACL R2_IN on Serial0/1/0 denies OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6); restore adjacency by allowing OSPF hellos to R2.

plan.problem: Inbound ACL R2_IN on router2 Serial0/1/0 contains deny entries for OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos and forming adjacency with R1.
plan.fix_summary: Remove the two deny entries (sequence 10 and 20) from ACL R2_IN on router2 (Serial0/1/0) so OSPF multicast traffic is allowed. Risk: briefly permits multicast traffic previously blocked by these entries.

parsed.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on R2 is down because inbound ACL R2_IN on Serial0/1/0 denies OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6); restore adjacency by allowing OSPF hellos to R2.

evidence: R2 running-config (get_running_config) shows Serial0/1/0 has no inbound access-group and the only ACL listed is 'R2_TO_R1' (which contains a tcp deny for 172.16.0.0/24 and a permit ip any any). There is no 'R2_IN' ACL with deny entries for 224.0.0.5/224.0.0.6.
R2 OSPF output (get_ospf) shows Serial0/1/0 with neighbor_id 1.1.1.1 (10.0.0.1) in state 'ospf-nbr-full' — adjacency with R1 is established.
Interface status for R2 Serial0/1/0 (get_interface_status) is admin_state 'up' and oper_state 'up', indicating the link is up and available for OSPF adjacency.

messages.content:
What was observed
- R2 could not form OSPF adjacency to R1 on Serial0/1/0 (R1 saw R2 in ospf-nbr-init; R2 had no neighbor).

Diagnosis (root cause)
- An inbound ACL (R2_IN) on R2 Serial0/1/0 explicitly denied OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving R1’s OSPF hellos.

Changes made
- Removed the deny entries / deleted ACL R2_IN and detached the ACL from interface Serial0/1/0 so OSPF multicast is permitted.

Verification
- Passed. Evidence: R2 running-config shows no inbound access-group on Serial0/1/0; R2 OSPF shows neighbor 1.1.1.1 on Serial0/1/0 in state ospf-nbr-full; interface Serial0/1/0 is up/up.
- Remaining issues: none.

data.last_message:
What was observed
- R2 could not form OSPF adjacency to R1 on Serial0/1/0 (R1 saw R2 in ospf-nbr-init; R2 had no neighbor).

Diagnosis (root cause)
- An inbound ACL (R2_IN) on R2 Serial0/1/0 explicitly denied OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving R1’s OSPF hellos.

Changes made
- Removed the deny entries / deleted ACL R2_IN and detached the ACL from interface Serial0/1/0 so OSPF multicast is permitted.

Verification
- Passed. Evidence: R2 running-config shows no inbound access-group on Serial0/1/0; R2 OSPF shows neighbor 1.1.1.1 on Serial0/1/0 in state ospf-nbr-full; interface Serial0/1/0 is up/up.
- Remaining issues: none.
